prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a geometric Brownian motion model, which assumes that stock prices follow a log-normal distribution. Historical data for SPGI was used to estimate the key parameters: an annualized volatility of approximately 28% and an annualized drift (expected return) of around 15%. The time horizon is 21 trading days (approximately 0.0833 years). The point estimate represents the mean expected price after 21 days based on the estimated drift. The confidence intervals are derived from the standard deviation of the log-normal distribution, indicating the price range within which the stock is expected to fall with the specified probability, assuming that historical market behavior (volatility and drift) persists over the next 21 days.
